results.question: Why did Chipotle's labor costs change?

claims.claim: Labor costs increased by 17.2% for the three months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023.
Labor costs increased by 15.1% for the six months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023.

Labor costs decreased as a percentage of total revenue by 0.2% for the three months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023, due to 1.5% sales leverage, partially offset by 1.1% restaurant wage inflation, including a 0.5% increase due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

Labor costs decreased as a percentage of total revenue by 0.3% for the six months ended June 30, 2024, compared to the same period in 2023, due to 1.3% sales leverage, partially offset by 0.8% restaurant wage inflation, including a 0.3% increase due to the minimum wage for restaurants like Chipotle in California increasing to $20 per hour in April 2024.

results.question: Why did marketing costs change at Darden?

claims.claim: Marketing expenses increased from $144.5 million in fiscal 2023 to $169.9 million in fiscal 2024, representing a 17.6% increase.
The increase was driven by a 22.1% increase in marketing expenses as a percentage of sales from continuing operations, from 1.1% to 1.3%.
Marketing expenses increased from $169.9 million in fiscal 2024 to $175.1 million in fiscal 2025, representing a 3.0% increase.
The increase was driven by a 3.0% increase in marketing expenses as a percentage of sales from continuing operations, from 1.3% to 1.4%.
Marketing expenses for the first quarter of fiscal 2025 were $169.9 million, representing a 1.5% increase compared to the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.
The increase was driven by a 1.5% increase in marketing expenses as a percentage of sales from continuing operations, from 1.3% to 1.4%.
